terms.definition: Expressing numeric array work as whole-array NumPy operations executed in compiled code rather than per-element Python loops.
A function or expression that applies to entire ndarrays at once, such as ufuncs, slice assignments, or axis reductions.
NumPy rules that align arrays of different shapes for element-wise ops by treating size-1 dimensions as stretchable along that axis.
A NumPy function that operates element-wise on arrays in compiled code, optionally with broadcasting and an out parameter.
Interpreter cost paid on every iteration when transforming array elements with explicit for-loops instead of vectorized kernels.
Memory arrangement where elements are stored in C-order without gaps, enabling fastest access for NumPy ufuncs and BLAS calls.
An array of True/False values used to select or update elements without branching inside Python loops.
